findings.title: Implement SSO/SAML Integration
findings.description: Integrate SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC to support enterprise onboarding. This integration must work with Okta, Azure AD, and OneLogin, supporting both IdP-initiated and SP-initiated flows with JIT provisioning.
assumptions: Enterprise customers require SSO for compliance.
Integration with major IdPs is feasible.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise prospects require SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC before signing contracts.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an enterprise user WHEN they attempt to log in THEN they can authenticate using SSO via SAML 2.0 or OIDC.

findings.title: Implement Multi-Tenant Data Isolation
findings.description: Remediate the current data isolation model to ensure compliance with SOC 2 Type II by implementing schema or database-level separation instead of application-level row filtering.
assumptions: Data isolation is essential for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Security audit flagged that tenant data isolation relies on application-level row filtering.

recommendations: Conduct a security audit to evaluate current isolation methods.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a security audit WHEN evaluating data isolation THEN it must show schema-level separation for each tenant.

findings.title: Enhance Role-Based Access Control
findings.description: Develop a fine-grained role-based access control system that allows for custom role definitions, permission inheritance, resource-level access controls, and audit logging of permission changes.
assumptions: Enterprise customers need more granular control over permissions.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers need fine-grained RBAC beyond our current admin/member/viewer model.

recommendations: Design a new RBAC model with user feedback.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an admin user WHEN they create a new role THEN they can define custom permissions and set inheritance rules.

findings.title: Implement Data Residency Controls
findings.description: Establish data residency controls that allow enterprise customers to choose where their data is stored, ensuring compliance with GDPR and data sovereignty requirements.
assumptions: Data residency is critical for enterprise customers in regulated industries.

evidence.excerpt: GDPR and data sovereignty requirements mean enterprise customers must choose where their data is stored.

recommendations: Implement region-pinned storage with cross-region replication disabled.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a customer account WHEN they select a data region THEN their data must be stored in the specified region with no cross-region replication.

findings.title: Implement White-Label Branding Features
findings.description: Develop white-label branding options that allow enterprise customers to customize the platform with their own logos, color schemes, and domain names.
assumptions: Enterprise customers desire customization for branding.

evidence.excerpt: Several enterprise prospects are looking for white-label branding options.

recommendations: Explore design options for white-label branding.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a customer account WHEN they configure branding settings THEN they can upload logos and select color schemes.

findings.title: Establish Customer Satisfaction Measurement
findings.description: Implement a systematic approach to collect customer satisfaction scores for enterprise tenants to understand user experience and improve retention.
assumptions: Customer satisfaction data is essential for retention.

evidence.excerpt: Currently, there is no systematic collection of customer satisfaction scores for enterprise tenants.

recommendations: Design a customer satisfaction survey and integrate it into the platform.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a customer account WHEN they complete a satisfaction survey THEN their feedback is recorded and analyzed.

findings.title: Optimize Onboarding Process
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on the onboarding process for enterprise tenants to identify methods that lead to faster onboarding times and higher satisfaction.
assumptions: Improving onboarding will enhance customer satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct A/B testing on the onboarding process for enterprise tenants.

recommendations: Implement A/B testing framework for onboarding.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN two onboarding processes WHEN new users complete onboarding THEN measure time taken and satisfaction scores.

findings.title: Monitor Platform Uptime
findings.description: Implement monitoring and alerting for platform uptime to ensure high availability and customer satisfaction.
assumptions: High uptime is critical for customer retention.

evidence.excerpt: This guardrail metric tracks the uptime of the platform over the last 90 days.

recommendations: Set up a monitoring dashboard for uptime metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a monitoring system WHEN uptime drops below 99.9% THEN an alert is triggered to the operations team.

findings.title: Collect User Feedback
findings.description: Implement a mechanism to collect user feedback on the platform's features and usability to guide future enhancements.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for continuous improvement.

evidence.excerpt: Currently, there is no systematic collection of customer satisfaction scores for enterprise tenants.

recommendations: Create a feedback form accessible from the user dashboard.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user account WHEN they access the feedback form THEN they can submit their feedback on platform features.

findings.title: Conduct Security Audit
findings.description: Perform a comprehensive security audit to identify vulnerabilities and ensure compliance with industry standards.
assumptions: Regular security audits are necessary for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Security audit flagged that tenant data isolation relies on application-level row filtering.

recommendations: Schedule regular security audits and address identified vulnerabilities.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a security audit schedule WHEN an audit is conducted THEN all vulnerabilities must be documented and addressed.

findings.title: Implement Audit Logging for Permissions
findings.description: Create an audit logging system that tracks all changes to permissions and roles within the platform to ensure accountability and compliance.
assumptions: Audit logging is essential for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Requirements include custom role definitions, permission inheritance, resource-level access controls, and audit logging of all permission changes.

recommendations: Design and implement an audit logging framework.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a role change occurs WHEN the change is made THEN it must be logged with user details and timestamp.

findings.title: Evaluate Compliance with Data Residency
findings.description: Assess current data storage practices to ensure compliance with data residency regulations, particularly for EU and APAC regions.
assumptions: Compliance with data residency regulations is mandatory.

evidence.excerpt: Affects database, file storage, and search indices.

recommendations: Conduct a compliance review of data storage practices.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a compliance review is conducted WHEN data storage practices are evaluated THEN they must meet GDPR and data sovereignty requirements.

summary: The analysis has resulted in a comprehensive set of requirements focused on enhancing security, compliance, and user experience for the EnterpriseSuite platform. Each requirement is tied to customer insights and metrics, ensuring alignment with enterprise needs and priorities.
